flags.stage_neg1_fallback: stage_neg1 requested but pivot budget exhausted (NEG1_PIVOT_BUDGET=3): case 6b — Stage 0.5 reject is conjecture-level (novelty), not derivation: reviewer explicitly states the proposal targets a field-level sharp bound, concedes it is not a published open-regime claim and not a named-estimator equivalence frontier, and that the flagship-style external-comparator non-representability clause of prop:lee-gamma-frontier-partial is not proved. Regression pattern (a) is moot (single attempt); the kernel itself was substituted (proposal_promise_gap=kernel_substituted), so re-deriving on the same .tex would still fail the orchestrator-enforced flagship floor. Pivot to a new angle instead of burning Stage 0 budget on an admittedly sub-flagship conjecture.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the average treatment effect under bounded collider bias: a closed-form quadratic identified set for the ATE when an analyst conditions (intentionally or by selection) on a partially-determined post-treatment outcome / mediator / selection indicator, characterized by two extremal joint laws (one saturating the upper envelope, one saturating the lower envelope) of the unobserved structural-error pair, with a non-trivial quadratic-in-the-collider-bias-parameter envelope strictly tighter than the Manski no-assumption bounds whenever any bias bound is finite; extends Greenland (2003) and VanderWeele (2009) collider-bias outer bounds to the sharp identified-set characterization, the direct open direction explicitly left in both papers' discussion sections
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue comparators remain pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 for budgeted selection envelopes, pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 for nonrectangular post-treatment principal-strata LPs, and the failed pid_mediation_proxy_v1 warnings about explicit endpoint construction. The proposal differs by targeting Lee always-selected principal strata with a finite selected-mixture odds-ratio budget Gamma and reverse-selection budget kappa, and by requiring explicit alpha-coupled endpoint laws rather than a generic LP sharpness claim.","published_axis":"Closest published comparators are Lee2009SampleSelection, ShepherdGilbertJemiaiRotnitzky2006, JemiaiRotnitzkyShepherdGilbert2007, Imai2008TruncationDeath, Chiba2012SACE, HuberMellace2015SharpSelection, LaffersNedela2017Sensitivity, Semenova2025GeneralizedLee, Okamoto2025RobustifyLee, and HonoreHu2020Selection. The patched epsilon-gap is the coupled sharp endpoint profile in Conjecture 1: armwise log-odds sensitivity can reproduce selected tables, but the common alpha equality plus H <= kappa p0 determines joint attainability and the sharp ATE frontier."}
seed_list: Prior work Greenland2003ColliderBias would predict that collider-stratification bias can be quantified as a magnitude assessment. Our seed conjectures a sharp finite-Gamma ATE interval with endpoint-attaining latent-error laws, because the odds-ratio collider table excludes specific Manski endpoint faces rather than merely bounding bias size.

Prior work BalkePearl1997Bounds has established sharp closed-form bounds from finite response-type programs. Our seed conjectures that post-treatment collider conditioning yields a curved odds-ratio frontier with two quadratic structural-error endpoint laws, because collider dependence is imposed after selection rather than as randomized noncompliance response types.

Prior work HernanHernandezDiazRobins2004Selection would predict that selection bias is structurally conditioning on a common effect. Our seed conjectures an observable sharp frontier without a primitive bridge law, because the selected law plus finite collider odds-ratio budget pins down excluded outcome-collider cells.

Prior work VanderWeeleRobins2009MonotonicEffects would imply qualitative signed-edge conclusions under monotonic effects. Our seed conjectures that finite collider dependence admits sharp numeric sign-change regimes, because endpoint laws can saturate opposite sides of the odds-ratio envelope while preserving the same signed DAG.

Prior work SmithVanderWeele2019Selection would predict practical selection-bias bounding factors. Our seed conjectures that those factors have a sharp additive ATE sensitivity curve underneath, because finite Gamma removes exactly the selected-sample completions that put all missing mass at the Manski endpoints.

Prior work MastenPoirierZhang2024Sensitivity establishes scalar sensitivity bounds that approach no-assumption bounds. Our seed conjectures the collider analogue has equality cases and strict-tightening certificates, because finite collider dependence excludes endpoint faces even when no unconfoundedness relaxation is imposed.

Prior work Lee2009SampleSelection establishes sharp trimming bounds under monotone treatment-induced selection. Our seed conjectures a trimming-free sharp interval under finite collider dependence, because selection-helped and selection-harmed latent types coexist instead of being removed from one tail.

Prior work FrangakisRubin2002PrincipalStratification would shift post-treatment-variable problems to principal-stratum estimands. Our seed conjectures that the marginal ATE remains sharply bounded after conditioning, because a common collider-error coupling can attain marginal endpoints without redefining the target.

Prior work CaiKurokiPearlTian2008DirectEffects establishes tight direct-effect bounds with confounded intermediates. Our seed conjectures that total ATE distortion from conditioning on the intermediate has different endpoint geometry, because the intermediate is a collider in the observed sampling rule rather than a controlled mediator level.

Prior work DingMiratrix2015MBias derives linear M-bias formulae. Our seed conjectures a nonparametric bounded-outcome sharp envelope that reduces to the linear formula only in the Gaussian-linear limit, because the finite-support collider table creates endpoint laws absent from coefficient algebra.

Prior work Huber2020DirectIndirectSelection identifies mediation effects with sample selection under stronger assumptions. Our seed conjectures a joint mediator-selection collider set with separate Gamma budgets, because the mediator and selection indicators share hidden coordinates that prevent rectangular endpoint pasting.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Greenland2003ColliderBias would predict that collider-stratification bias can be quantified as a magnitude assessment. Our seed conjectures a sharp finite-Gamma ATE interval with endpoint-attaining latent-error laws, because the odds-ratio collider table excludes specific Manski endpoint faces rather than merely bounding bias size.

anchor_paper.author: Sander Greenland
anchor_paper.claim: Quantifies collider-stratification bias under simple causal models, but treats it as magnitude assessment rather than a sharp ATE identified set.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; the mechanism is endpoint-face exclusion under collider odds-ratio saturation, not a simple assumption weakening.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Greenland2003ColliderBias because the paper already quantifies collider bias magnitudes.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Greenland gives magnitude calculations and examples, not an observed-law-complete sharp ATE set with endpoint-attaining laws.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BalkePearl1997Bounds has established sharp closed-form bounds from finite response-type programs. Our seed conjectures that post-treatment collider conditioning yields a curved odds-ratio frontier with two quadratic structural-error endpoint laws, because collider dependence is imposed after selection rather than as randomized noncompliance response types.

anchor_paper.author: Alexander Balke and Judea Pearl
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Shows response-type programs can yield sharp closed-form ATE bounds in binary causal systems, but not for post-treatment collider conditioning with curved dependence restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/sharpness-failure pattern not blocked because the named target is Balke-Pearl sharp endpoint construction and the proposed obstruction is an open class of post-treatment collider laws, not a hand-tuned witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BalkePearl1997Bounds because every finite binary causal problem can be written as a response-type LP.
seed_details.rebuttal: False as stated for the flagship: the finite-Gamma odds-ratio restriction is curved, and the novelty is closed-form endpoint realization under that restriction, not generic finite enumeration.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HernanHernandezDiazRobins2004Selection would predict that selection bias is structurally conditioning on a common effect. Our seed conjectures an observable sharp frontier without a primitive bridge law, because the selected law plus finite collider odds-ratio budget pins down excluded outcome-collider cells.

anchor_paper.author: Miguel A. Hernan, Sonia Hernandez-Diaz, and James M. Robins
anchor_paper.claim: Frames selection bias structurally as conditioning on a common effect of variables related to exposure and outcome, but does not solve the latent-error optimization problem.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; the surprise is observable endpoint construction without a primitive bridge or conditioning law.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from structural selection-bias DAGs because the common-effect graph already states the bias mechanism.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the DAG states why bias occurs but not the sharp numerical set over compatible missing outcome cells.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work VanderWeeleRobins2009MonotonicEffects would imply qualitative signed-edge conclusions under monotonic effects. Our seed conjectures that finite collider dependence admits sharp numeric sign-change regimes, because endpoint laws can saturate opposite sides of the odds-ratio envelope while preserving the same signed DAG.

anchor_paper.author: Tyler J. VanderWeele and James M. Robins
anchor_paper.claim: Develops monotonic-effect and signed-edge DAG tools for qualitative bias conclusions, leaving exact numerical optimization over compatible latent laws outside the result.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern not blocked because the proposed phase boundary is a sign-change regime under finite Gamma, not a generic monotonicity relaxation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from VanderWeeleRobins2009MonotonicEffects because signed edges determine the direction of conditional associations.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their results are qualitative and assumption-specific; the proposed object is a sharp numeric ATE interval with finite-dependence endpoints.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SmithVanderWeele2019Selection would predict practical selection-bias bounding factors. Our seed conjectures that those factors have a sharp additive ATE sensitivity curve underneath, because finite Gamma removes exactly the selected-sample completions that put all missing mass at the Manski endpoints.

anchor_paper.author: Louisa H. Smith and Tyler J. VanderWeele
anchor_paper.claim: Gives simple selection-bias bounding factors, including additive-scale supplements, but these are robustness outer bounds rather than sharp feasible-set characterizations.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/changes assumptions pattern not blocked because the new phenomenon is sharp endpoint-face exclusion and equality cases, not a broader hypothesis class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from SmithVanderWeele2019Selection because additive selection-bias bounds already give the needed sensitivity curve.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: bounding factors are outer robustness tools and do not construct endpoint-attaining laws or prove sharpness of the ATE set.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MastenPoirierZhang2024Sensitivity establishes scalar sensitivity bounds that approach no-assumption bounds. Our seed conjectures the collider analogue has equality cases and strict-tightening certificates, because finite collider dependence excludes endpoint faces even when no unconfoundedness relaxation is imposed.

anchor_paper.author: Matthew A. Masten, Alexandre Poirier, and Linqi Zhang
anchor_paper.claim: Estimates ATE and ATT bounds under scalar relaxations of unconfoundedness that approach no-assumption bounds as the sensitivity value grows.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method M from S1 to S2 pattern not blocked because the collider frontier has a different post-treatment endpoint geometry and equality-case certificate, not a direct unconfoundedness sensitivity transplant.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MastenPoirierZhang2024Sensitivity because scalar sensitivity curves already cover ATE bounds approaching Manski.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their scalar relaxation targets unconfoundedness, while this seed targets conditioning-induced collider dependence and missing outcome-collider cells.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Lee2009SampleSelection establishes sharp trimming bounds under monotone treatment-induced selection. Our seed conjectures a trimming-free sharp interval under finite collider dependence, because selection-helped and selection-harmed latent types coexist instead of being removed from one tail.

anchor_paper.author: David S. Lee
anchor_paper.claim: Derives sharp trimming bounds for treatment effects when outcomes are observed only after nonrandom sample selection affected by treatment, relying on monotonicity of selection.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Generalizes monotonicity pattern not blocked because finite collider dependence changes the geometry from tail trimming to two-sided latent-type coexistence.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Lee2009SampleSelection because sample-selection ATE bounds are already sharp.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Lee sharpness relies on monotone selection and tail trimming; the finite-Gamma collider budget permits both signs of selection response and needs different endpoint laws.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FrangakisRubin2002PrincipalStratification would shift post-treatment-variable problems to principal-stratum estimands. Our seed conjectures that the marginal ATE remains sharply bounded after conditioning, because a common collider-error coupling can attain marginal endpoints without redefining the target.

anchor_paper.author: Constantine E. Frangakis and Donald B. Rubin
anchor_paper.claim: Introduces principal stratification for causal effects in the presence of post-treatment variables, but shifts the estimand rather than sharply bounding the full-population ATE after realized conditioning.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation pattern not blocked because retaining the marginal ATE reveals an identification gap hidden by principal-effect redefinition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from principal stratification because post-treatment variables are handled by principal strata.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: principal stratification changes the estimand; it does not provide a sharp marginal ATE set after realized collider conditioning.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CaiKurokiPearlTian2008DirectEffects establishes tight direct-effect bounds with confounded intermediates. Our seed conjectures that total ATE distortion from conditioning on the intermediate has different endpoint geometry, because the intermediate is a collider in the observed sampling rule rather than a controlled mediator level.

anchor_paper.author: Zhihong Cai, Manabu Kuroki, Judea Pearl, and Jin Tian
anchor_paper.claim: Derives tight bounds for average controlled direct effects with unmeasured intermediate-outcome confounding, not for total ATE distortion induced by conditioning on the intermediate.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation pattern not blocked because the switch from controlled direct effect to total ATE distortion exposes a different conditioning-induced identification gap.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from CaiKurokiPearlTian2008DirectEffects because symbolic Balke-Pearl programs already bound post-treatment mediator effects.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their target is ACDE under mediator-outcome confounding, not the full-population ATE after conditioning on the mediator as collider.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DingMiratrix2015MBias derives linear M-bias formulae. Our seed conjectures a nonparametric bounded-outcome sharp envelope that reduces to the linear formula only in the Gaussian-linear limit, because the finite-support collider table creates endpoint laws absent from coefficient algebra.

anchor_paper.author: Peng Ding and Luke W. Miratrix
anchor_paper.claim: Derives formulae for when adjustment for an M-structure inflates or reduces bias, mainly in linear structural equation models.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method M from S1 to S2 pattern not blocked because the extension changes the object from linear coefficient bias to sharp nonparametric endpoint laws.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DingMiratrix2015MBias because M-bias formulas already quantify collider adjustment bias.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: linear structural formulas do not characterize the full bounded-outcome counterfactual feasible set under finite dependence.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Huber2020DirectIndirectSelection identifies mediation effects with sample selection under stronger assumptions. Our seed conjectures a joint mediator-selection collider set with separate Gamma budgets, because the mediator and selection indicators share hidden coordinates that prevent rectangular endpoint pasting.

anchor_paper.author: Martin Huber
anchor_paper.claim: Identifies direct and indirect effects under sample selection or attrition assumptions and leaves combined sample-selection and mediator-endogeneity extensions as future research.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method M from S1 to S2 pattern not blocked because the proposed multivariate collider set acquires nonrectangular hidden-coordinate compatibility constraints absent in the single-selection setting.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Huber2020DirectIndirectSelection because mediation with selection is already handled under identification assumptions.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Huber uses identifying restrictions; the seed weakens to bounded collider dependence and asks for a sharp partial-ID set over joint latent errors.

proposed_from.literature_map:
1. Greenland (2003, Epidemiology) is the central collider-stratification anchor: it quantifies possible bias magnitudes but not a sharp ATE identified set.
2. Hernan, Hernandez-Diaz, and Robins (2004, Epidemiology) give the structural selection-bias DAG in which conditioning on a common effect is the shared mechanism.
3. VanderWeele and Robins (2009, JMLR) provide signed-edge and monotonic-effect tools for qualitative bias conclusions, not sharp numeric intervals.
4. Smith and VanderWeele (2019, Epidemiology) give practical selection-bias bounding factors, including additive supplements, but as outer robustness bounds.
5. Manski (1990, AER) is the no-assumption benchmark that finite collider-bias information should strictly tighten.
6. Balke and Pearl (1997, JASA) show finite response-type programs can yield sharp closed-form treatment-effect bounds in binary systems.
7. Lee (2009, Review of Economic Studies) gives sharp sample-selection trimming bounds under monotone selection; finite collider dependence is a different restriction.
8. Frangakis and Rubin (2002, Biometrics) define principal stratification for post-treatment variables but usually change the estimand rather than bound the marginal ATE after conditioning.
9. Cai, Kuroki, Pearl, and Tian (2008, Biometrics) bound direct effects with confounded intermediates, adjacent to but distinct from total ATE distortion by conditioning.
10. Ding and Miratrix (2015, Journal of Causal Inference) analyze M-bias in linear structural models; the nonparametric sharp-bound analogue remains open.
11. Masten, Poirier, and Zhang (2024, JBES) provide scalar sensitivity-indexed ATE/ATT bounds for relaxed unconfoundedness; the analogous collider-conditioning sensitivity curve is missing.
12. Huber (2020, Econometrics) treats mediation with sample selection under identifying assumptions and points to harder combined mediator-endogeneity and selection extensions.
13. The named tension is between qualitative or outer-bound collider-bias tools and econometric sharp partial-identification objects.
14. The common missing theorem is a finite-parameter ATE set attained by explicit extremal latent-error laws and reducing to Manski only as the bias bound becomes infinite.

banked_reason: Split D0 honest: 3 conj verdicts (lee-gamma-frontier=partial; strict-tightening,coupling-iff=confirmed). D0.5 reject NOT solver-side: proposal headlined Greenland/VanderWeele collider envelope but §8 conjectures are Lee-Gamma framed and no §8 conjecture proves the headlined external-comparator clause. Proposal drift detected by upgraded split solver; needs proposal-stage upgrade not solver retry.
